headline: School politics class: US Congress approves bill to release Jeffrey Epstein files
body_text:
This article picked by a teacher with suggested questions is part of the Financial Times free schools access programme. Details/registration here.

Read our full range of politics picks here.

In a move which indicates that his control over the Republican Party in Congress is not ironclad, Donald Trump has reversed his position on the publication of files concerning the late sex offender, Jeffrey Epstein. Trump had originally opposed their release by the Department of Justice.

However, faced with growing calls from some of his supporters for transparency, he performed a U-turn and urged Congress to vote for the documents to be disclosed. The House of Representatives voted by 427 to one to release the files and the Senate followed unanimously without taking a vote.

Click the link below to read the article and then answer the questions:

US Congress approves bill to release Jeffrey Epstein files

Question in the style of Edexcel Politics Paper 3A

Question in the style of AQA Politics
